editor.title: Editor
editor.subtitle: Build your own cases
editor.name: Case name
editor.windowHint: Click near the edge of a tile to place a window there. Click again to remove it.
editor.doorHint: Click near the edge of a tile to place a door there (counts from both sides). Click again to remove it.
editor.suspectsSoon: Suspects & clues come in the next stage.
editor.saveHint: 'Keep' stores it in the browser (shows up under 'Open case'). 'As JSON' downloads a file — drop it into the levels folder.
editor.nameTaken: A level with this name already exists — overwrite?
editor.resultOkNoMurderer: Solvable & unique, but no single murderer.
editor.result_multi: Ambiguous — more than one solution. It can't be saved like this.
editor.result_none: Not solvable — no valid arrangement. It can't be saved like this.
editor.result_contradiction: Only solvable via contradictions — make it solvable by pure logic before saving.
editor.result_aborted: Check aborted — no valid solution is in sight on this board (the victim must be alone with exactly one suspect). Adjust rooms/clues.
editor.needBoard: Build a playable board first — at least two rooms.
editor.randomBoardHint: Generate fresh rooms and objects (suspects kept)
editor.result_genfail: No solvable level found — adjust the board or try again.
editor.result_genfailVorgaben: No unique level found with these constraints — loosen them (allow more clue types or free the targets).
editor.randomConstrainedHint: Use only the chosen clue types
editor.constraintsIntro: Pick the clue types the generator may use. “🎲 Generator picks” leaves object/trait/room to the generator (it always picks the concrete person anyway); turn it off to fix your chosen targets. Suspects are generated using only these types.

tutorial.lockRestart: That would restart the case — handy in a real game. Here, press »Next« for the second part.
tutorial.lockBack: That would go back to the level list — but then you'd miss the second part! Press »Next«.

err.notCandidate: No, not here — that's not a possible tile for {{name}}.
err.holdToPlace: Press and hold for ~1 second to place (not just a tap).
err.tapForNote: Here just tap briefly to leave a note.
err.crossWrong: Not that tile — only block the highlighted ones.
err.markOnly: Just small notes here — don't place yet.

welcome.title: Welcome, detective!
welcome.body: Last night there was a murder. Let's solve this case together — I'll guide you step by step. Tap »Next«.

goal.title: Your goal
goal.body: Find the murderer: work out who was in the same room as the victim. Whoever is alone with the victim is the murderer.

rules.title: The rules
rules.body:
• Each person is in a different row AND column.
• Only beds, chairs and carpets can be occupied.
• Tables, TVs, plants, shelves and boxes are blocked.
• The victim is on the last free tile.

board.title: The crime scene
board.body: This is the crime scene — a 4×4 grid with two rooms: Living Room (top) and Bedroom (bottom). Hover over an object to see its name.
board.body_touch: This is the crime scene — a 4×4 grid with two rooms: Living Room (top) and Bedroom (bottom). Tap an object to see its name.

suspects.title: The suspects
suspects.body: Here are the suspects with their statements: Alex was beside a table, Bernd on a bed, Carmen by a window.
suspects.body_touch: Below the board are the suspects with their statements: Alex was beside a table, Bernd on a bed, Carmen by a window.

terms.title: Bold = important
terms.body: Objects and key words are bold. Hover over a word like »beside« or »alone« to see exactly what it means.
terms.body_touch: Objects and key words are bold. Tap a word like »beside« or »alone« to see exactly what it means.

selectA.title: Select Alex
selectA.body: Let's start with Alex. Tap his card to select him.

noteA.title: Where can Alex be?
noteA.body: Alex was beside a table — the blue-outlined tiles are possible. Tap them all briefly to note a small A there.

selectB.title: Select Bernd
selectB.body: On to Bernd. Tap his card.

noteB.title: Where can Bernd be?
noteB.body: Bernd was on a bed — that's walkable. Briefly tap the blue-outlined beds to note a small B.

placeC.title: Place Carmen
placeC.body: Carmen must be by a window — only ONE tile is left! Press and hold that tile for ~1 second to place her there.

locked.title: Row & column locked
locked.body: Look closely: Carmen's row and column are now crossed out — nobody else can stand there. That rules out one of the two beds for Bernd.

selectB2.title: Select Bernd again
selectB2.body: Now Bernd's bed is clear. Select Bernd again.

placeB.title: Place Bernd
placeB.body: Only one bed is possible now. Press and hold it for ~1 second to place Bernd.

selectA2.title: Select Alex
selectA2.body: And now Alex — only one tile is left for him too. Select Alex.

placeA.title: Place Alex
placeA.body: Place Alex on the only remaining tile beside the table (press and hold for ~1 second).

tools.title: Your tools
tools.body: Quick tour of the tools: X tool (block tiles), Reset (hold), Undo, Hint and Solve.
tools.body_touch: Quick tour of the tools in the bar at the bottom: X tool (block tiles), Reset (hold), Undo, Hint and Solve.

selectV.title: Select the victim
selectV.body: Only the victim is left. Tap Olli (the victim).

placeV.title: Place the victim
placeV.body: The victim is on the last free tile. Press and hold it for ~1 second to place Olli.

solve.title: Solve!
solve.body: Done — everyone is placed! Now press »Solve«. Whoever is alone with the victim is the murderer.

verdict.title: Case closed — now what?
verdict.body: Congratulations! Here's the verdict. You can »reopen« the case (solve it again from scratch) or tap aside to look at the solved board once more — tap the board and the verdict comes back. In real cases you can also jump straight to the next level from here. Press »Next« — there's one more important thing to learn.

w_intro.title: Second case: The Flat
w_intro.body: Another case — this time you'll learn to cross out (block) tiles yourself. It helps a lot with narrowing down. Tap »Next«.

w_selectE.title: Select Emil
w_selectE.body: Let's start at the bottom with Emil. Tap Emil's card.
w_selectE.body_touch: Let's start at the bottom with Emil. Scroll down through the suspects (below the board) and tap Emil's card.

w_rowE.title: Emil is in row 6
w_rowE.body: Emil was beside a table AND in row 6 — the three blue tiles are possible for him. Since only ONE person stands per row, nobody else is in row 6. So the two black-outlined tiles (R6/C5, R6/C6) can go — you'll block them yourself in a moment.

w_armX.title: Activate »Block cell«
w_armX.body: Tap »X« / Block cell at the top right. It lets you mark tiles as impossible yourself.
w_armX.body_touch: Tap »X« / Block cell in the bar at the bottom. It lets you mark tiles as impossible yourself.

w_crossE.title: Cross out tiles
w_crossE.body: Now tap the two black-outlined tiles in row 6 (R6/C5 and R6/C6). They get an X: nobody can stand there anymore.

w_selectA.title: Select Armin
w_selectA.body: Now select Armin — tap his card.
w_selectA.body_touch: Now scroll back up through the suspects and select Armin.

w_noteA.title: Where can Armin be?
w_noteA.body: Armin was in the kitchen (top right). New trick: press a blue tile and drag — without lifting — across the other blue-outlined kitchen tiles. That drops a small A on all of them at once. (Tapping each one briefly also works.)

w_selectB.title: Select Bella
w_selectB.body: On to Bella. Tap her card.

w_noteB.title: Where can Bella be?
w_noteB.body: Bella was in the kitchen too, and in a corner. Note the blue corner tiles with a small B.

w_rowsAB.title: Nobody else in rows 1 & 2
w_rowsAB.body: Armin and Bella are BOTH in the kitchen — so in rows 1 and 2. That takes both rows: nobody else can stand there. So the black-outlined tiles to the left are empty — you'll block them in a moment.

w_crossAB.title: Cross out the left
w_crossAB.body: Tap the black-outlined tiles on the left. They all get an X. (The tile with the plant is blocked anyway — no need to cross it.)

w_placeC.title: Place Caro
w_placeC.body: Caro was on a chair. Every other chair is crossed out or sits in rows 1–2 (Armin/Bella) — only R4/C3 is left! That's how crossing out helps. Press and hold the tile for ~1 second to place Caro.

w_hintBtn.title: The hint button
w_hintBtn.body: Ever stuck? Try the Hint button — press it once.

w_hintShown.title: A hint appears
w_hintShown.body: Above the suspects there's now a hint — it tells you what to cross out next, or which person can be placed and why. So you're never stuck. But use it sparingly: solving it yourself is more fun. Every level is solvable by pure logic.

w_settingsBtn.title: Open settings
w_settingsBtn.body: The gear at the top right — tap it to open the settings.

w_settingsOpen.title: Tune the help
w_settingsOpen.body: Here you set how much help you get — from lots of hints to none at all. Try it out. A real master detective needs no help — but decide for yourself! Then tap »Next«.

w_selectD.title: Finish up: Diana
w_selectD.body: Let's place the rest together. Select Diana.

w_placeD.title: Place Diana
w_placeD.body: Diana was on a toilet — there's only one (R5/C1). Press and hold it for ~1 second.

w_placeE.title: Emil is clear
w_placeE.body: Diana (column 1) and Caro (column 3) block two of his tiles, and you crossed out R6/C5 and R6/C6 — only R6/C4 is left. Place Emil there (press and hold ~1 second).

w_placeB.title: Bella's corner
w_placeB.body: Armin is north of Bella — so Bella must be lower: the corner R2/C6. Place Bella (press and hold ~1 second).

w_placeA.title: Place Armin
w_placeA.body: Armin's only tile left is R1/C5 — in the kitchen, north of Bella. Press and hold the tile for ~1 second.

w_selectV.title: The victim
w_selectV.body: Only the victim is left. Tap their card.

w_placeV.title: Place the victim
w_placeV.body: The victim is on the last free tile (R3/C2). Press and hold it for ~1 second.

w_solve.title: Solve the case!
w_solve.body: Everyone is placed. Press »Solve« — whoever is alone with the victim is the murderer. Good luck with your own cases!

generate.title: Generate a level
generate.subtitle: Compose your case
generate.hint: Larger boards and "Hard" can take a few seconds.
generate.failed: No level found — please try again or pick different settings.

settings.title: Settings
settings.helpFullSub: Marks every cell the statements still allow.
settings.helpReducedSub: Shows leads only: mentioned objects, rooms and traces.
settings.helpNoneSub: No markings — you deduce entirely on your own.

game.selectPrompt: Tap to select · long-press to place
game.waterWalkable: Water areas are walkable – someone can stand there too.
game.author: A case by {{name}}

tool.uncrossWhy: Someone can stand here – your cross is wrong.
tool.placeAllElse: No other cell is left for {{name}}.
tool.placeFromNotesWhy: Your notes leave only this cell.
tool.reasonsTogglePlace: Why the other cells drop out

result.loseBody: At least one person is misplaced. Keep at it!
result.peekHint: Tap aside to view the scene
result.nameLabel: What should the level be called?

clue.uniqueNearWindow: {{subject}} was {{neg}}[[the only person beside a window:uniqueNearWindow]]
clue.uniqueNearDoor: {{subject}} was {{neg}}[[the only person beside a door:uniqueNearDoor]]

roomAttribute.someMin: {{subject}} was {{neg}}in a room with [[at least:roomAttrMin]] {{count}} other [[suspects:suspect]] who had {{attribute}}
roomAttribute.someExact: {{subject}} was {{neg}}in a room with [[exactly:roomAttrExact]] {{count}} other [[suspects:suspect]] who had {{attribute}}

clue.roomExistsCorner: In {{poss}} room there was {{mateLc}} [[in a corner:corner]]
clue.roomExistsWall: In {{poss}} room there was {{mateLc}} [[at a wall:atWall]]
clue.roomExistsWindow: In {{poss}} room there was {{mateLc}} [[by a window:nearWindow]]
clue.roomExistsDoor: In {{poss}} room there was {{mateLc}} [[by a door:nearDoor]]
clue.directionFromAttrTraitSome: {{subject}} was {{neg}}{{direction}} of at least [[one suspect:suspect]] who had {{attribute}}
clue.insideXor: {{subject}} and {{target}} were neither both [[inside:inside]] nor both [[outside:outside]]

rule.aloneWithVictim: The victim must be alone in a room with exactly one suspect.
rule.oneEachLine: Each person must stand in their own row and column.

tip.negation: Negation – flips the condition to its opposite: what follows is exactly NOT the case.
tip.onObject: Standing on this object's tile (it is walkable).
tip.nearObject: Right next to it — same room, one tile left, right, above or below the object.
tip.nearWindow: On the tile right at a window (the window sits on that tile's wall).
tip.corner: Corner tile: at least two walls touch / enclose the tile.
tip.atWall: At least one side of the tile borders a wall (board edge or another room).
tip.alone: Truly alone in the room — no other suspect, and not the victim either.
tip.aloneWith: Only this one person is otherwise in the room (the victim doesn't count).
tip.uniqueOnObject: The only person standing on such an object.
tip.uniqueNearObject: The only person standing beside such an object.
tip.uniqueNearWindow: The only person standing beside a window.
tip.uniqueNearDoor: The only person standing beside a door.
tip.uniqueInside: The only person who is inside.
tip.uniqueOutside: The only person who is outside.
tip.sameRoom: In the same room as this person.
tip.sameRoomAsObject: In the same room where such an object stands.
tip.besideSameObject: Beside the same object instance as this person/someone — multi-tile objects (table, bed …) count as one.
tip.aloneSameRoom: Just the two of them in the room — no one else, not even the victim.
tip.aloneSameRoomAsObject: Alone in the room with this object — no other person, not even the victim.
tip.direction: Lies in this compass direction from the named person (north = further up, etc.).
tip.offset: Exactly that many tiles in this direction from the target person.
tip.roomAttrNone: Nobody else in the same room has this trait.
tip.roomAttrSome: At least one person in the same room has this trait.
tip.roomAttrMin: At least this many people in the same room have this trait.
tip.roomAttrExact: Exactly this many people in the same room have this trait — no more, no fewer.
tip.roomAttrAll: Everyone in the same room has this trait.
tip.inRow: The named row (counted from the top).
tip.inCol: The named column (counted from the left).
tip.nearDoor: On the tile right at a door (doors count from both sides).
tip.inside: In an indoor room (not pasture, yard or garden).
tip.outside: In an outdoor area (pasture, yard or garden).
tip.notAlone: At least one other person is in the same room.
tip.suspect: Only the suspects — the victim does not count here.
tip.person: All people: the suspects and the victim together.

cond.roomExists: With someone in the room (on/near object)
cond.aloneHint: Additionally alone — just the two of them (or alone with the object), no one else in the room.
cond.uniqueHint: Additionally the only person there — no one else.
cond.genFreeHint: On: the generator picks object/trait/room (and the person regardless). Off: your fixed targets apply.

step.nakedSingle: {{name}} must be on {{cell}} – the only possible cell.
step.uniqueConstraint: Only {{name}} may stand there – others excluded from those cells.
step.hiddenSingleRow: In row {{line}} everything is crossed out except {{cells}} – only {{name}} fits there, no other suspect and not the victim.
step.hiddenSingleCol: In column {{line}} everything is crossed out except {{cells}} – only {{name}} fits there, no other suspect and not the victim.
step.forcedCellRow: In row {{line}} only {{cell}} is free – someone must be there, so column {{perpLine}} is taken: the other cells of that column are crossed out.
step.forcedCellCol: In column {{line}} only {{cell}} is free – someone must be there, so row {{perpLine}} is taken: the other cells of that row are crossed out.
step.hiddenSingleRowVictim: In row {{line}} everything is crossed out except {{cells}} – only the victim ({{name}}) can be there, no suspect.
step.hiddenSingleColVictim: In column {{line}} everything is crossed out except {{cells}} – only the victim ({{name}}) can be there, no suspect.
step.crossCenter: {{name}} can only be in row {{row}} or column {{col}}; so their intersection {{cell}} can hold no one (not a cell for {{name}}, blocked for everyone else) – removed.
step.nakedGroupRows: {{people}}: only rows {{lines}} possible – blocked for others.
step.nakedGroupCols: {{people}}: only columns {{lines}} possible – blocked for others.
step.rectangle: Wherever {{name}} ends up, {{cell}} stays blocked – nobody can be there.
step.insideXor: {{other}} is {{otherSide}}, so {{name}} is not {{otherSide}} – cells excluded.
step.boardCountFull: Already {{count}} people on {{object}} – excluded for everyone else.
step.boardCountFull_one: Already {{count}} person on {{object}} – excluded for everyone else.
step.boardCountConfine: Exactly {{count}} people must be on {{object}}, and only that many can still be there – {{name}} is one of them and must go there.
step.boardCountConfine_one: Someone must be on {{object}}, and no one but {{name}} can still be there – so {{name}} must go there.
step.boardCountLine: Someone must be on {{object}}, and {{object}} only exists in {{line}} {{num}} – so that line's occupant is on it; the rest of the {{line}} is empty.
step.objectPigeonhole: At least {{count}} different people must be on {{object}}; if {{cell}} were taken there wouldn't be enough seats left for all of them – so {{cell}} stays empty.
step.boardCountLines: Exactly {{count}} people must be on {{object}}, and the possible cells lie in exactly {{count}} {{linePlural}} – so each of those {{linePlural}} has someone on it; every other cell of them is empty.
step.removePlacement: You placed {{name}} on {{cell}}, but {{name}} can't be there – please remove {{name}} here.
step.placeFromNotes: Per your notes, only {{cell}} is left for {{name}} – place {{name}} there.
step.notesWrong: Your notes leave only {{cell}} for {{name}}, but {{name}} can't be there at all – something is off, check your notes for {{name}}.
step.removeMarks: Your notes for {{name}} on {{cells}} can go – {{name}} can't be there. Remove those notes (don't cross the cell out).
step.placementWrong: Something's off: {{name}} can't be on {{cell}}. Please take {{name}} off and re-check the clues.
step.removeCross: Your crosses on {{cells}} are wrong – please remove them.
step.removeCross_one: Your cross on {{cells}} is wrong – please remove it.
step.emptyRoomsAllOccupied: Enough rooms are already occupied that the rest must stay empty (exactly {{count}} empty) – nobody can be here.
step.emptyRoomsConfine: The allowed empty rooms are already fixed ({{count}}) – so {{room}} must be occupied, and only {{name}} can be there.
step.emptyRoomsLine: No room stays empty, and {{room}} can only be filled in {{line}} {{num}} – so someone sits there; every other cell of that {{line}} outside {{room}} is empty.
step.relationalDirectionAttr: {{name}} is {{direction}} of someone with a matching trait – impossible cells removed.
step.relationalSameRoom: {{name}} is in the same room as {{target}} – other rooms removed.
step.sameObject: {{name}} must stand beside the same object ({{objectNom}}) as the named person/someone – impossible cells removed.
step.sameObjectForce: {{name}} needs someone beside {{objectSame}} – only {{target}} can be it, so they must go there.
step.differentRoom: {{name}} is not in the same room as {{target}} – {{target}}'s room is excluded for {{name}}.
step.aloneExcludeRoom: {{name}} must be alone, but someone is already in the {{room}} – so not there.
step.aloneExcludeLine: {{name}} must be alone, but {{line}} {{num}} lies entirely in the {{room}} and is occupied – so not there.
step.aloneReserve: {{name}} is alone in the {{room}} – everyone else is excluded from it.
step.notAloneRoom: {{name}} wasn't alone – cells whose room nobody else could join are removed.
step.notAloneForce: {{name}} wasn't alone in the {{room}} – only {{target}} can still join, so they must be there.
step.notAloneOccupied: {{name}} wasn't alone in the {{room}} – it needs ≥2 people, only possible with {{cell}} taken; rest of that row and column blocked.
step.roomCoverageReserve: Every room has exactly one suspect; {{name}} belongs in the {{room}} – others are excluded from it.
step.roomCoverageConfine: Only {{name}} can fill the {{room}}, and no room stays empty – {{name}} must go there.
step.emptyRoomForcing: If {{room}} were occupied someone would end up with no spot – so {{room}} must stay empty; nobody can be here.
step.roomAssignmentReserve: Apart from the suspects who must share a room, every room has exactly one of its own – {{name}} belongs in the {{room}}, others excluded from it.
step.roomAssignmentConfine: Only {{name}} is left to be the {{room}}'s own occupant – so {{name}} must go there.
step.companionRoom: {{name}} was alone with exactly {{count}} matching people – the {{room}} doesn't fit, so not there.
step.companionRoom_one: {{name}} was alone with exactly the matching person – the {{room}} doesn't fit, so not there.
step.companionRoomBusy: {{name}} was alone with exactly {{count}} matching people – but the {{room}} surely already holds {{sure}} others, so not there.
step.companionRoomBusy_one: {{name}} was alone with exactly one matching person – but the {{room}} surely already holds {{sure}} others, so not there.
step.companionForce: {{name}} is in {{room}} alone with exactly {{count}} matching people – only matching suspects qualify, so {{target}} must go there.
step.companionForce_one: {{name}} is in {{room}} alone with exactly one matching person – only {{target}} qualifies, so {{target}} must go there.
step.companionReserve: {{name}} is in {{room}} alone with only matching company – everyone else (and the victim) must leave the {{room}}.
step.companionPairing: {{count}} people each need a matching companion in separate rooms – only {{people}} can cover that, so they are confined to those rooms.
step.companionFit: {{name}} can't be there – the required companion doesn't fit in the room too (row/column, chair, or reserved cells).
step.roomExistsRoom: Someone matching had to be {{pos}} in {{name}}'s room; the {{room}} can't, so not there.
step.roomExistsSpot: Someone must be {{pos}} in {{name}}'s room ({{room}}) – only {{cell}} can do that. The cell is certainly taken: the rest of its row and column is blocked, the cell itself only fits possible companions.
step.roomExistsOccupant: The person {{pos}} in {{name}}'s room can only be {{target}} – everyone else is ruled out, so {{target}} must be on {{cell}}.
step.roomExistsOnlyMatcher: Someone matching had to be {{pos}} in {{name}}'s room; only {{target}} can be (everyone else is ruled out) – so {{target}} is in the same room as {{name}}.
step.aloneWithRoom: {{name}} must share a room with the named person(s) – other rooms removed.
step.aloneWithReserve: {{name}} was only with the named people – the victim and non-matching people are excluded from the {{room}}.
step.roomCapacityFull: The {{room}} is already full – no one else can be there.
step.roomCapacityPair: {{name}} and {{target}} must be together, but the {{room}} has no space for both – so not there.
step.roomBijection: No room stays empty: {{people}} must split the rooms (one each) – given the layout, {{name}} can't be in the {{room}}.
step.attrSomeRoom: {{name}}'s room must hold at least {{count}} matching people – the {{room}} can't, so not there.
step.attrSomeRoom_one: {{name}}'s room must hold a matching person – the {{room}} can't, so not there.
step.attrExactRoom: {{name}}'s room must hold exactly {{count}} matching people – the {{room}} can't, so not there.
step.attrExactRoom_one: {{name}}'s room must hold exactly one matching person – the {{room}} can't, so not there.
step.attrForce: {{name}}'s room needs {{count}} matching people – only {{target}} can be one of them, so {{target}} must go to {{room}}.
step.attrAllRoom: Everyone in {{name}}'s room must match – the {{room}} has someone who doesn't, so not there.
step.attrExcludeRoom: No one in {{name}}'s room may have {{attribute}}; the {{room}} has such a person – so not there.
step.attrExcludeRoomGender: {{name}}'s room must have {{whoNeg}}; the {{room}} has {{who}} – so not there.
step.attrPigeonhole: No one in {{name}}'s room may have {{attribute}}; the {{room}} is bound to have such a person (too many for the other rooms) – so not there.
step.attrPigeonholeGender: {{name}}'s room must have {{whoNeg}}; the {{room}} is bound to have {{who}} (too many for the other rooms) – so not there.
step.attrReserve: {{name}} is in the {{room}}, which must have no {{attribute}} – such people are excluded.
step.attrReserveGender: {{name}} is in the {{room}}, which must have {{whoNeg}} – such people are excluded.
step.groupRoomMemberOut: {{name}}'s group must share one room, but a member can't be in the {{room}} – the group isn't there.
step.groupRoomForeign: Someone who can't belong to {{name}}'s group is certainly in the {{room}} – the group isn't there.
step.groupRoomNoExtra: No fitting companion for {{name}}'s group can be in the {{room}} – the group isn't there.
step.groupRoomCapacity: {{name}}'s group ({{size}} people) doesn't fit into the {{room}} – the group isn't there.
step.groupRoomMember: Only {{target}} can be {{name}}'s companion in the {{room}} – so {{target}} must be there.
step.groupRoomFull: {{name}}'s companions in the {{room}} are complete – everyone else leaves the {{room}}.
step.groupRoomDir: {{target}} is {{name}}'s companion and must be {{direction}} of {{name}} – impossible cells removed.
step.groupRoomDirExtra: Anyone in the {{room}} with {{name}} is the companion and must be {{direction}} of {{name}} – {{target}}'s unfitting cells removed.
step.groupRoomDirSubject: A possible companion must stand {{direction}} of {{name}} – {{name}}'s cells without one are removed.
step.caseSplitContradiction: Quick check: {{name}} on {{cell}} leads straight to a contradiction – cell ruled out.
step.shortExclude: {{name}} can't be on {{cell}} – otherwise someone is left with no cell in the end:
step.caseSplitCommon: Case split: wherever {{name}} stands ({{count}} options), some cells are impossible either way – removed.
step.murderVictimNotRoom: The {{room}} already holds {{count}} suspects – the victim wasn't there (they were alone with the murderer).
step.murderRoomFill: The {{room}} is exactly full (victim alone with the murderer) – one required cell there can only be {{name}}.
step.murderIdentified: The victim is in the {{room}}; {{name}} is there too – so {{name}} is the murderer, everyone else leaves the {{room}}.
step.murderConfine: The victim is in the {{room}}; only {{name}} can be there – {{name}} must go there (alone with the victim).
step.murderNoMurderer: {{name}} can't be there – otherwise the victim is nowhere left alone with exactly one suspect (no clear murderer).
step.murderNoVictimCell: The victim can't be there – there it wouldn't be alone with exactly one suspect.
step.alreadyPlaced: {{name}} is already on {{cell}} – so {{name}} can't be on {{cells}}.
step.forcing: Assuming {{name}} on {{cell}} leads to a contradiction – cell excluded.
step.satPlace: {{name}} must be on {{cell}} – every other cell leads to a contradiction.
step.satExclude: {{count}} cells lead to a contradiction for {{name}} – excluded.
step.satExclude_one: {{count}} cell leads to a contradiction for {{name}} – excluded.
step.victim: The victim {{name}} remains on the last free cell {{cell}}.
step.murderer: Murderer: {{name}} was alone with the victim in the {{room}}.
step.murdererAmbiguous: No unique murderer: {{count}} suspects in the {{room}}.
step.stuck: Stuck: {{count}} people unresolved (technique not yet implemented).
step.stuck_one: Stuck: {{count}} person unresolved (technique not yet implemented).
step.contradiction: Dead end: this arrangement breaks a clue — there is no valid solution.

contra.empty: {{name}} would then have no possible cell left.
contra.murder: the victim could then no longer be alone with anyone.
contra.general: the arrangement then no longer works out.
contra.exhausted: the rest then can't be completed (all possibilities checked).

why.onlyRow: {{cell}}: not here – only {{name}} can be in row {{line}}, the others are fixed elsewhere (e.g. {{bound}}).
why.onlyCol: {{cell}}: not here – only {{name}} can be in column {{line}}, the others are fixed elsewhere (e.g. {{bound}}).
why.aloneOccupied: {{name}} must be alone here, but {{target}} is already in this room.
why.rowTaken: {{target}} is already in this row – {{name}} can't be here.
why.colTaken: {{target}} is already in this column – {{name}} can't be here.
why.rowConfined: Per your notes, {{target}} can only be in row {{num}} – no one else can be there.
why.colConfined: Per your notes, {{target}} can only be in column {{num}} – no one else can be there.
why.crossConfined: Per your notes, {{target}} can only be in row {{rowNum}} or column {{colNum}} – anyone on {{cell}} would leave {{target}} with no cell.
why.lastCandidate: Only {{name}} could still go on {{cells}} – everyone else is already ruled out there.
why.lastCandidateVictim: Only the victim could still go on {{cells}} – no suspect can be there.
why.crossThis: → no one can be here: cross out {{cells}}.
